name: Databricks Photon
summary: Spark's native C++ execution engine (SIGMOD 2022): vectorised interpretation with SIMD-friendly kernels. ~80% of fully-compiled-code performance without per-query JIT compilation latency. Selective codegen for hot paths supplements vectorisation. Reaches the same point HyPer aims at without the JIT overhead.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Photon** (Databricks, SIGMOD 2022) is Apache Spark's native C++ execution engine, a full rewrite of Spark's SQL execution layer. Unlike HyPer's per-query codegen, Photon uses a **vectorised interpreter**: operations process batches of rows at a time with SIMD-friendly kernels, amortising dispatch overhead across the batch.

The architectural choice is deliberate: vectorised interpretation reaches ~80% of the performance of fully compiled code while avoiding per-query compilation latency. For workloads that mix many short queries with a few long-running ones, vectorisation is the better default. Photon supplements this with selective codegen for hot paths.

sections.title: Industry convergence
sections.content:
Photon, DuckDB, and Velox illustrate a broader convergence: the best analytical query engines of 2024 are neither pure interpreters nor pure compilers, but vectorised engines with optional codegen. The dominant axis of optimisation has become **batching + SIMD**, not instruction-per-tuple compilation.

The lesson: vectorisation captures most of compilation's benefits without compilation's compile-time cost. For workloads where many distinct queries run, this trade-off is overwhelmingly favourable.
